text:
There once was a teenage girl named Lily who lived in a sleepy town. One sunny afternoon, while playing hide-and-seek under the shade of an old oak tree, she discovered a peculiar patch of earth. It wasn't dirt—that couldn't explain the shiny, otherworldly flowers growing there. The petals shimmered with colors that weren't quite imaginable. That's when Lily noticed a small figure among the flowers.

The tiny being moved gracefully, almost dancing, and it had feathers instead of arms. As the peculiar flower creature approached, its voice tinkled like bells. "Hello, human!" it chirped.

Lily stared. "You're... You're not real?"

"No!" The creature laughed. "I am! Here in this enchanted patch! Welcome to the Moonflower Meadows."

Curiosity piqued, Lily asked how she got there. The bird explained it was a magic place. Moonflowers bloom only once every century, granting wishes and speaking to dreamers. Lily wished
